automated_metrics.fabrication: FAIL — only orchestrator.md metadata table (allowed machine-generated)
automated_metrics.overlap: FAIL — max pairwise 78.4% (dev vs qa); architect vs dev 71.4%

tradeoff: Round 3 expanded views to fix functional completeness per round 2 QA feedback (qa needs BA-QA fail gates + acceptance criterion, dev needs storage layout + failure strategy + Reference Resolution Rule, orchestrator needs feature flags + command coverage + maintenance scheme). This increased cross-cutting content sharing, regressing overlap/uniqueness metrics.

unresolved_issues.title: dev vs qa overlap 78.4%
unresolved_issues.detail: Round 3 added BA Reference Resolution Rule + failure strategy + storage layout to dev.md (for implementation) and BA-QA fail gates + failure strategy to qa.md (for enforcement). These overlap because both agents need to know the rules being implemented/enforced.

unresolved_issues.title: architect vs dev overlap 71.4%
unresolved_issues.detail: Architecture Dual Touchpoint and Storage Layout sections needed by both architect (design) and dev (implementation).

unresolved_issues.title: qa.md uniqueness 13.7%
unresolved_issues.detail: qa.md is small (51 lines) and most content (acceptance criterion, fail gates, failure strategy) is cross-cutting. Inherent to small spec with multi-agent dependencies.

decision: Proceed with round 3 split. Functional completeness (agent self-sufficiency) is prioritized over overlap metrics for this 240-line spec where cross-agent dependencies are inherent.
